text.title: nginx Reverse Proxy: Core Directives
text.summary: nginx reverse proxy configuration: proxy_pass routing, WebSocket upgrade map, proxy headers snippet, and upstream blocks. Architecture: Internet → Cloudflare → nginx (80/443) → backend on 127.0.0.1:PORT.

rule.statement: When stripping a URL prefix, use rewrite + break — not a trailing slash on proxy_pass.
rule.rationale: A trailing slash on proxy_pass causes nginx to rewrite the URI internally, but rewrite with break gives explicit control and avoids double-slash bugs when the prefix is multi-segment.

section.title: WebSocket Upgrade Map
section.p: Define the map block in the http context (nginx.conf or conf.d/), then include websocket.conf in WebSocket locations.

section.title: Proxy Headers
section.p: All proxy locations must forward these headers so backends see the real client IP and protocol.

rule.statement: Always hardcode X-Forwarded-Proto as https when Cloudflare proxies traffic.
rule.rationale: Cloudflare sends HTTP to the origin even when the browser used HTTPS. Backends rely on X-Forwarded-Proto to generate correct redirect and cookie URLs.

section.title: Upstream Blocks
section.p: Use upstream blocks when multiple backends need load balancing or when the backend address appears in more than one location.
